level_signals: 5+ years as Technical Program Manager or technical leadership role
Base salary range $211,200–$264,000 consistent with senior IC at a top AI company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience as a Technical Program Manager or in a technical leadership role managing complex, large-scale software engineering or machine learning development projects.
2+ years of dedicated experience managing programs focused directly on core engineering infrastructure, platform services, or distributed systems.
Strong foundational understanding of the Generative AI lifecycle, including LLM utilization for structured downstream tasks, model fine-tuning, and performance evaluation.
Proven track record of presenting to and influencing executive-level stakeholders, with the ability to translate complex technical challenges into clear business impacts.
Advanced proficiency with iterative development methodologies and modern project management tooling (Jira, Linear, etc.).
An insatiable appetite for learning and deeply engaging with modern ML/GenAI practices and infrastructure.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong software engineering fundamentals, ideally with prior professional experience as a software engineer or data developer before transitioning into program management.
Proven success driving the internal adoption of technical platforms, SDKs, or APIs across disparate product lines or independent business units.
Direct experience working with data quality pipelines, LLM-as-a-judge evaluation frameworks, or automated RLHF systems.

scope.surface: Frontier Agent Engineering / enterprise customer engagements — agentic application delivery at enterprise scale
scope.business_model: B2B / B2G enterprise AI platform and services
primary_metrics_inferred: Program health metrics and delivery forecasts
core_responsibilities: Own strategic planning, scheduling, and high-velocity execution of multiple enterprise-grade programs
Run weekly cross-functional syncs, surface blockers, drive decisions
Manage complex dependencies and technical communication across core teams
Synthesize deep technical complexities into actionable insights for engineers and C-suite stakeholders
Proactively identify, track, and architect mitigations for technical risks
Modernize and scale agile execution frameworks
Define, track, and report on key program health metrics directly to executive leadership

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($211,200–$264,000 base) for SF, NYC, and Seattle
Equity mentioned as part of total compensation
High-impact, high-ownership role with direct executive exposure
Clear articulation of responsibilities and scope
Well-known AI company (Scale AI) with prominent enterprise and government customers
red_flags: No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
High buzzword density may obscure actual day-to-day scope
Role appears to require onsite presence in SF, NYC, or Seattle; remote eligibility not stated
90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role — limits re-application flexibility
Compensation note says range 'may be inclusive of several career levels' — actual level placement is opaque
